published_prior_art.cite: Kitagawa2015: finite discrete-IV validity/monotonicity testable implications; this is an infinitesimal all-cell analogue
HeckmanVytlacil2005 and Vytlacil2002: LIV/MTE derivative and threshold-crossing equivalence under monotonicity
ChesherRosen2017 gives general GIV sharp-set machinery but not a fixed-b scalar continuous-IV derivative-path support certificate

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_ordinal_late_partial_defiers_v1: zig-zag compatibility strictly tightens adjacent pairwise bounded-defier bounds

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The strict nonsharpness target is a generic locally pasted derivative-envelope workflow; KennedyLorchSmall2019 estimates a monotone LIV curve and does not publish the bounded-violation adjacent-cell pasting method being refuted, so tier=letter below novelty_target=flagship unless a real published target is named.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The §8 comparison calls KennedyLorchSmall2019 an applied continuous-IV sensitivity/local-pasting workflow, but the paper develops semiparametric doubly robust estimation of the local IV curve under monotonicity, not independent bounded-violation derivative envelopes.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey omits KilbertusKusnerSilva2020, a published NeurIPS continuous-IV causal-bounds algorithm paper, and Gunsilius2019, a close working-paper path-space continuous-IV bounds comparator; neither subsumes the kernel, but both are closer than several listed finite-IV comparators.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The support-function gap uses an unspecified dual norm and product-space topology for the derivative-measure process, so Delta_b(P) and finite-threshold separation are not mathematically pinned.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: K_b is defined in §6 as the set generated by feasible full-data laws, making the headline 'model nonempty iff r(P) in K_b' tautological; the conjecture needs an explicit constructive K_b or a projection-equals-certificate statement.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The switcher subdistribution intensities are only pointwise bounded in y; they also need CDF/measure structure in y, otherwise the claimed all-cell local relaxation is too broad for the Kitagawa-style reduction.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the KennedyLorchSmall2019 straw target with a specific published method that actually pastes local continuous-IV or bounded-violation cells, or downgrade Conjecture 2 to an internal CausalSmith comparison.
Define the Banach/topological vector space for r(P), the dual unit ball, and the finite-threshold subspace used by sigma_K_b and Delta_b.
Add y-monotonicity, right-continuity, and endpoint constraints for each alpha subdistribution intensity, then restate the discrete Kitagawa corner using those measure constraints.
Add a related-work paragraph comparing KilbertusKusnerSilva2020 and Gunsilius2019 to the fixed-b binary-treatment derivative-path certificate.

recommended_next_step: Revise the statement layer: make K_b constructive, specify the support-function topology, add subdistribution-in-y constraints, and either name a real published local-pasting target for Conjecture 2 or remove its flagship role.
